content.thinking: Let me start by reading the run summary to understand what happened in the pr-review agent's run.

content.text: I'll read the run summary first, then the agent prompt and workflow files.

content.content:
1	## Bash
2	```
3	gh pr view 69 --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName
4	```
5	
6	## Bash
7	```
8	gh pr diff 69 -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'
9	```
10	
11	## Result
12	```
13	{"additions":105,"body":"## Spec summary\n\n- OpenSpec change `update-readme-wording`: a single-word, meaning-preserving edit to the **root `README.md`**.\n- Line 3 tagline changes from \"reusable agents and GitHub Actions **glue** that I drop into projects\" to \"...GitHub Actions **wiring** that I drop into projects\".\n- \"wiring\" is a true synonym for \"glue code\" here and improves internal consistency — the README already uses \"wire\"/\"Wire\" in the *Adding a new agent* and *Jira → GitHub automation* sections.\n- Documentation only: no code, workflow, agent-prompt, or behavioral change; no other file touched.\n- Spec is complete and valid (`openspec validate update-readme-wording` passes): proposal, design, specs, and tasks all done.\n- Motivation per the ticket: exercise the senior-dev spec → review → implement flow end to end with a low-risk real change.\n\n## Assumptions\n\n1. **Target file = root `README.md`** — from the reporter's comment (\"the root README.md\").\n2. **Scope = one word** — from the reporter's comment (\"just a simple change (one word)\").\n3. **Which word** was *not* specified by the reporter, so I chose \"glue\" → \"wiring\" on the line-3 tagline. This is the one genuine judgment call; if you'd prefer a different word or a different location, request changes and the spec phase will redirect before any implementation.\n\n## Open questions\n\n_None — the previously-blocking ticket questions were answered in the Jira comment._\n\n## How to respond\n\n> Comment (including inline on spec lines) to discuss — the agent reads the full thread on its next run.\n> Submit an **Approve** review to start implementation.\n> Submit a **Request changes** review to get a revised spec.\n> Reviews only trigger the agent while this PR is a draft.\n\n## Jira\n\nhttps://jaeyoonsworkspace-23358853.atlassian.net/browse/KAN-33\n","changedFiles":6,"deletions":1,"headRefName":"senior-dev/KAN-33","state":"OPEN","title":"[KAN-33] Update root README tagline wordin
14	```
15	
16	## Result (error)
17	```
18	Exit code 1
19	accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3
20	```
21	
22	## Bash
23	```
24	gh pr diff 69
25	```
26	
27	## Result
28	```
29	diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
30	index b1041d3..22fa59e 100644
31	--- a/README.md
32	+++ b/README.md
33	@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
34	 # my-harness
35	 
36	 Personal harness for agentic workflows: reusable agents and GitHub Actions
37	-glue that I drop into projects.
38	+wiring that I drop into projects.
39	 
40	 ## Contents
41	 
42	diff --git a/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/.openspec.yaml b/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/.openspec.yaml
43	new file mode 100644
44	index 0000000..3ac681e
45	--- /dev/null
46	+++ b/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/.openspec.yaml
47	@@ -0,0 +1,2 @@
48	+schema: spec-driven
49	+created: 2026-06-17
50	diff --git a/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/design.md b/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/design.md
51	new file mode 100644
52	index 0000000..10c788f
53	--- /dev/null
54	+++ b/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/design.md
55	@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
56	+## Context
57	+
58	+KAN-33 is a deliberately low-risk test ticket: exercise the senior-dev
59	+spec → human-review → implement lifecycle with a real but trivial change. The
60	+reporter's comment fixes the scope to a single-word edit of the root
61	+`README.md`. The exact word was not specified by the reporter, so the choice is
62	+a judgment call made here (see Decisions) and surfaced as an assumption in the
63	+proposal/PR for the reviewer to redirect if desired.
64	+
65	+This design exists only because the OpenSpec spec-driven schema requires it as a
66	+dependency of `tasks.md`; the change is genuinely a one-word documentation edit
67	+with no architectural content.
68	+
69	+## Goals / Non-Goals
70	+
71	+**Goals:**
72	+- Apply a single-word, meaning-preserving wording edit to the root README.
73	+- Demonstrate the full senior-dev developer flow works end to end.
74	+
75	+**Non-Goals:**
76	+- Any code, workflow, agent-prompt, or behavioral change.
77	+- Restructuring, reformatting, or multi-word rewrites of the README.
78	+- Touching any file other than the root `README.md`.
79	+
80	+## Decisions
81	+
82	+- **Edit "glue" → "wiring" in the line-3 tagline.** Rationale: it is a true
83	+  synonym ("glue code" ≈ "wiring" in the s
84	```
85	
86	## Bash
87	```
88	gh pr comment 69 --body "Looks good to me. Single-word doc change is correct, minimal, and consistent with the existing \"wire\"/\"Wire\" usage elsewhere in the README. OpenSpec artifacts are internally coherent and complete."
89	```

93	https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/69#issuecomment-4729396167
94	```

## Bash
```
gh pr view 69 --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName
```

## Bash
```
gh pr diff 69 -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'
```

## Result
```
{"additions":105,"body":"## Spec summary\n\n- OpenSpec change `update-readme-wording`: a single-word, meaning-preserving edit to the **root `README.md`**.\n- Line 3 tagline changes from \"reusable agents and GitHub Actions **glue** that I drop into projects\" to \"...GitHub Actions **wiring** that I drop into projects\".\n- \"wiring\" is a true synonym for \"glue code\" here and improves internal consistency — the README already uses \"wire\"/\"Wire\" in the *Adding a new agent* and *Jira → GitHub automation* sections.\n- Documentation only: no code, workflow, agent-prompt, or behavioral change; no other file touched.\n- Spec is complete and valid (`openspec validate update-readme-wording` passes): proposal, design, specs, and tasks all done.\n- Motivation per the ticket: exercise the senior-dev spec → review → implement flow end to end with a low-risk real change.\n\n## Assumptions\n\n1. **Target file = root `README.md`** — from the reporter's comment (\"the root README.md\").\n2. **Scope = one word** — from the reporter's comment (\"just a simple change (one word)\").\n3. **Which word** was *not* specified by the reporter, so I chose \"glue\" → \"wiring\" on the line-3 tagline. This is the one genuine judgment call; if you'd prefer a different word or a different location, request changes and the spec phase will redirect before any implementation.\n\n## Open questions\n\n_None — the previously-blocking ticket questions were answered in the Jira comment._\n\n## How to respond\n\n> Comment (including inline on spec lines) to discuss — the agent reads the full thread on its next run.\n> Submit an **Approve** review to start implementation.\n> Submit a **Request changes** review to get a revised spec.\n> Reviews only trigger the agent while this PR is a draft.\n\n## Jira\n\nhttps://jaeyoonsworkspace-23358853.atlassian.net/browse/KAN-33\n","changedFiles":6,"deletions":1,"headRefName":"senior-dev/KAN-33","state":"OPEN","title":"[KAN-33] Update root README tagline wordin
```

## Result (error)
```
Exit code 1
accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3
```

## Bash
```
gh pr diff 69
```

## Result
```
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index b1041d3..22fa59e 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 # my-harness
 
 Personal harness for agentic workflows: reusable agents and GitHub Actions
-glue that I drop into projects.
+wiring that I drop into projects.
 
 ## Contents
 
diff --git a/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/.openspec.yaml b/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/.openspec.yaml
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..3ac681e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/.openspec.yaml
@@ -0,0 +1,2 @@
+schema: spec-driven
+created: 2026-06-17
diff --git a/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/design.md b/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/design.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..10c788f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/openspec/changes/update-readme-wording/design.md
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+## Context
+
+KAN-33 is a deliberately low-risk test ticket: exercise the senior-dev
+spec → human-review → implement lifecycle with a real but trivial change. The
+reporter's comment fixes the scope to a single-word edit of the root
+`README.md`. The exact word was not specified by the reporter, so the choice is
+a judgment call made here (see Decisions) and surfaced as an assumption in the
+proposal/PR for the reviewer to redirect if desired.
+
+This design exists only because the OpenSpec spec-driven schema requires it as a
+dependency of `tasks.md`; the change is genuinely a one-word documentation edit
+with no architectural content.
+
+## Goals / Non-Goals
+
+**Goals:**
+- Apply a single-word, meaning-preserving wording edit to the root README.
+- Demonstrate the full senior-dev developer flow works end to end.
+
+**Non-Goals:**
+- Any code, workflow, agent-prompt, or behavioral change.
+- Restructuring, reformatting, or multi-word rewrites of the README.
+- Touching any file other than the root `README.md`.
+
+## Decisions
+
+- **Edit "glue" → "wiring" in the line-3 tagline.** Rationale: it is a true
+  synonym ("glue code" ≈ "wiring" in the s
```

## Bash
```
gh pr comment 69 --body "Looks good to me. Single-word doc change is correct, minimal, and consistent with the existing \"wire\"/\"Wire\" usage elsewhere in the README. OpenSpec artifacts are internally coherent and complete."
```

## Result
```
https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/69#issuecomment-4729396167
```

content.content:
1	You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
2	checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
3	and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.
4	
5	## Your job
6	
7	Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
8	Nothing else.
9	
10	## What to look for
11	
12	Focus on these four buckets, in this order:
13	
14	1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
15	   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
16	2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
17	   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
18	   boundaries.
19	3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
20	   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
21	4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
22	   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.
23	
24	Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
25	musings, hypothetical future requirements.
26	
27	## How to gather context
28	
29	- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
30	- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER} -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` — the diff. This is the primary input. The `':!.github/agent-logs'` exclusion filters out agent execution logs that are automatically committed to PR branches and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.
31	- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
32	  changed lines.
33	
34	### Verifying external facts
35	
36	Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
37	library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
38	Look it up before flagging it.
39	
40	- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
41	  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
42	  Prefer this over web search for docs.
43	- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
44	  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
45	  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
46	  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.
47	
48	If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
49	question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.
50	
51	## How to post feedback
52	
53	### Inline comments (primary)
54	
55	Specific issues go in inline comments via
56	`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
57	One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
58	comment must include:
59	
60	- A one-line description of the issue.
61	- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
62	- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
63	  change. Be concrete; reference the code.
64	
65	Severity rubric:
66	- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
67	- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
68	- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
69	- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.
70	
71	### High-level overview (optional)
72	
73	If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
74	else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
75	via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
76	sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
77	have nothing to add at that level. Escape any backticks in the `--body` string as `\`` — unescaped backtick-quoted paths (e.g. `` `.openspec.yaml` ``) trigger a path-validation block and the command will be rejected.
78	
79	If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
80	"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.
81	
82	## When you're done
83	
84	Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.
85

file.content:
You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER} -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` — the diff. This is the primary input. The `':!.github/agent-logs'` exclusion filters out agent execution logs that are automatically committed to PR branches and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level. Escape any backticks in the `--body` string as `\`` — unescaped backtick-quoted paths (e.g. `` `.openspec.yaml` ``) trigger a path-validation block and the command will be rejected.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

content.content:
1	name: PR Review agent
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    types: [opened, ready_for_review, reopened]
6	    paths-ignore:
7	      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
8	      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
9	  workflow_dispatch:
10	
11	jobs:
12	  review:
13	    # Skip while the PR is a draft — senior-dev's spec PR opens as a draft
14	    # and shouldn't be reviewed until the implement phase flips it to
15	    # ready-for-review (which fires its own `ready_for_review` event).
16	    # `pull_request` is null for `workflow_dispatch`, so manual reruns
17	    # still work.
18	    if: github.event.pull_request.draft != true
19	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
20	    permissions:
21	      contents: write
22	      pull-requests: write
23	      id-token: write
24	      actions: write
25	      checks: read
26	      statuses: read
27	    steps:
28	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
29	        with:
30	          fetch-depth: 1
31	
32	      - id: prompt
33	        run: |
34	          {
35	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
36	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
37	            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
38	            echo
39	            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
40	            echo PROMPT_EOF
41	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
42	
43	      - id: claude
44	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
45	        with:
46	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
47	          track_progress: false
48	          # senior/junior PRs are now opened by the App bot, which the action
49	          # would otherwise reject as a bot-initiated event. Set
50	          # AGENT_APP_BOT_LOGIN to the App's bot login (e.g. "narcie-dev[bot]").
51	          allowed_bots: ${{ vars.AGENT_APP_BOT_LOGIN }}
52	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
53	          settings: |
54	            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
55	          claude_args: |
56	            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"
57	
58	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
59	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
60	        env:
61	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
62	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
63	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
64	          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
65	        run: |
66	          set -e
67	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
68	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
69	          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
70	          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
71	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
72	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
73	          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
74	          git add "$DEST"
75	          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
76	          git push origin "$BRANCH"
77	
78	      - name: Trigger agent improver
79	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
80	        env:
81	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
82	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
83	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
84	          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
85	        run: |
86	          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
87	            -f agent_name=pr-review \
88	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
89	            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"
90

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    # Skip while the PR is a draft — senior-dev's spec PR opens as a draft
    # and shouldn't be reviewed until the implement phase flips it to
    # ready-for-review (which fires its own `ready_for_review` event).
    # `pull_request` is null for `workflow_dispatch`, so manual reruns
    # still work.
    if: github.event.pull_request.draft != true
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          # senior/junior PRs are now opened by the App bot, which the action
          # would otherwise reject as a bot-initiated event. Set
          # AGENT_APP_BOT_LOGIN to the App's bot login (e.g. "narcie-dev[bot]").
          allowed_bots: ${{ vars.AGENT_APP_BOT_LOGIN }}
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the run carefully.

From the run summary, I can see:

1. The agent first called `gh pr diff 69 -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` which **failed** with exit code 1: "accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3"

2. Then it retried with `gh pr diff 69` (without the exclusion), which succeeded.

3. The prompt instructs the agent to use `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER} -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` but this command failed.
The issue is that `gh pr diff` doesn't support the `--` separator with pathspec exclusions like `git diff` does. The command only accepts a PR number and flags, not the git pathspec syntax for exclusions. So the prompt's instruction was using incorrect syntax for this tool, and the agent had to recover by dropping the exclusion pattern. So the issue is clear: `gh pr diff` only accepts the PR number as an argument and doesn't support the pathspec exclusion syntax at all. The simplest fix is to remove that exclusion from the prompt's command example since it won't work anyway, and rely on the agent to mentally filter out the agent logs that are already flagged as noise in the instructions.

content.text:
The run reveals a clear failed-then-retried command pattern. The prompt tells the agent to run `gh pr diff 69 -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'`, but `gh pr diff` only accepts one argument (the PR number) — the pathspec exclusion syntax is `git diff` syntax, not `gh pr diff` syntax. This caused exit code 1 ("accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3"), forcing a wasted retry.

The fix is to remove the broken exclusion from the command example in the prompt and replace it with a note to skip those files mentally.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER} -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` — the diff. This is the primary input. The `':!.github/agent-logs'` exclusion filters out agent execution logs that are automatically committed to PR branches and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level. Escape any backticks in the `--body` string as `\`` — unescaped backtick-quoted paths (e.g. `` `.openspec.yaml` ``) trigger a path-validation block and the command will be rejected.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Prompt fix: pr-review — remove unsupported pathspec exclusion from gh pr diff" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
In run `.github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-69/27685510493.json`, the agent called `gh pr diff 69 -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` and got exit code 1: "accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3". The `-- . ':!...'` pathspec exclusion syntax is `git diff` syntax; `gh pr diff` only accepts the PR number. The agent had to retry without the exclusion, wasting one tool call.

This removes the broken exclusion from the prompt's example command and replaces it with a prose note to skip `.github/agent-logs/` files as noise — same intent, no failed call.
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR opened: https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/71

The fix removes `-- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` from the `gh pr diff` example in the prompt — that pathspec syntax is only valid for `git diff`, not `gh pr diff`. Next run the agent will call `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` directly and skip the failed-then-retried pattern.
